title: Serino 2015 synchronous far audio-tactile PPS training
citation: Serino, A., Canzoneri, E., Marzolla, M., di Pellegrino, G., & Magosso, E. (2015). Extending peripersonal space representation without tool-use: evidence from a combined behavioral-computational approach. Frontiers in Behavioral Neuroscience, 9, 4.
notes: Training-induced PPS plasticity profile. The preload captures the measurement task: 3000 ms pink-noise IN/OUT sounds, right-hand tactile detection, T1-T5 SOAs, and auditory-only catch trials. The synchronous versus asynchronous training protocol is stored as metadata until ordered training phases become a first-class GUI concept.

reference_parameters.measurement_task: right-hand speeded tactile detection with task-irrelevant IN/OUT pink noise
reference_parameters.response_rule: vocal response to tactile target, ignore sound

provenance.source: Frontiers Methods report 3000 ms pink-noise IN/OUT sounds, right-hand tactile target detection, 77% target trials, and T1-T5 at 300, 800, 1500, 2200, and 2700 ms.
Frontiers article reports synchronous versus asynchronous far auditory stimulation paired with hand tactile stimulation.
